proof: function[T0, T1: FiniteGroup](x0: MulAction[T1, T0], x1: T0) { map[T1, T0](T1.elements, function(x2: T1) { x0.act(x2, x1) }).unique = finite_orbit_elements[T1, T0](x0, x1) }[X, G](a, x)

goal: exists(k0: G) { G.elements.contains(k0) and a.act(k0, x) = y }

proof: function[T0: FiniteGroup] { T0.elements.contains_every }[G]
function[T0](x0: List[T0]) { forall(x1: T0) { x1 ∈ x0 } = x0.contains_every }[G](G.elements)
function[T0](x0: T0 -> Bool) { not exists(k0: T0) { not x0(k0) } or not forall(x1: T0) { x0(x1) } }[G](G.elements.contains)
function[T0](x0: T0 -> Bool, x1: T0) { not forall(x2: T0) { x0(x2) } or x0(x1) }[G](G.elements.contains, g)

proof: let w0: X satisfy { w0 ∈ list_set[X](finite_orbit_elements[G, X](a, x)) != w0 ∈ orbit[G, X](a, x) }

goal: finite_action_fiber_set[G, X](a, x, y).contains(g) = finite_action_fiber_contains(a, x, y, g)
proof: function[T0: FiniteGroup, T1](x0: MulAction[T0, T1], x1: T1, x2: T1) { Set.new[T0](finite_action_fiber_contains[T0, T1](x0, x1, x2)) = finite_action_fiber_set[T0, T1](x0, x1, x2) }[G, X](a, x, y)

goal: finite_action_fiber_contains(a, x, y, g) = (a.act(g, x) = y)
proof: function[T0, T1: FiniteGroup](x0: MulAction[T1, T0], x1: T1, x2: T0, x3: T0) { x0.act(x1, x2) = x3 = finite_action_fiber_contains[T1, T0](x0, x2, x3, x1) }[X, G](a, g, x, y)

goal: c.is_disjoint(d) = forall(x0: G) { not (c.contains(x0) and d.contains(x0)) }
proof: function[T0](x0: Set[T0], x1: Set[T0]) { forall(x2: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or not x2 ∈ x1 } = x0.is_disjoint(x1) }[G](c, d)
let w0: G satisfy { w0 ∈ c and w0 ∈ d }
function(x0: G) { not x0 ∈ d or not x0 ∈ c }(w0)
function[T0](x0: List[Set[T0]]) { forall(x1: Set[T0], x2: Set[T0]) { not x1 ∈ x0 or not x2 ∈ x0 or x2 = x1 or x1.is_disjoint(x2) } = set_list_pairwise_disjoint[T0](x0) }[G](fibers)
let w0: Set[G] satisfy { exists(k0: Set[G]) { w0 ∈ fibers and k0 ∈ fibers and k0 != w0 and not w0.is_disjoint(k0) } }
let w1: Set[G] satisfy { w0 ∈ fibers and w1 ∈ fibers and w0 != w1 and not w0.is_disjoint(w1) }
let w2: Set[G] satisfy { w0 ∈ fibers and w2 ∈ fibers and w0 != w2 and not w0.is_disjoint(w2) }
function(x0: Set[G], x1: Set[G]) { not x0 ∈ fibers or not x1 ∈ fibers or x0.is_disjoint(x1) or x0 = x1 }(w0, w2)

goal: exists(k0: X) { items.contains(k0) and family(k0) = c }

goal: finite_action_fiber_set[G, X](a, x, y).cardinality_is(finite_stabilizer[G, X](a, x).order)
set_list_all_cardinality_is[G](fibers, finite_stabilizer[G, X](a, x).order) = forall(x0: Set[G]) { fibers.contains(x0) implies x0.cardinality_is(finite_stabilizer[G, X](a, x).order) }
proof: function[T0](x0: List[Set[T0]], x1: lib(nat.nat_base).Nat) { forall(x2: Set[T0]) { not x2 ∈ x0 or x2.cardinality_is(x1) } = set_list_all_cardinality_is[T0](x0, x1) }[G](fibers, finite_stabilizer[G, X](a, x).order)
let w0: Set[G] satisfy { w0 ∈ fibers and not w0.cardinality_is(finite_stabilizer[G, X](a, x).order) }
function(x0: Set[G]) { not x0 ∈ fibers or x0.cardinality_is(finite_stabilizer[G, X](a, x).order) }(w0)
let w0: G satisfy { not w0 ∈ union }
function(x0: G) { x0 ∈ union }(w0)
